task: I‚Äôm cleaning up a small labeling workflow, and I want to make sure I‚Äôm not setting myself up for a stupid mix-up later. Can you help me think through a simple way to organize the main set, the backups, and the notes so nothing gets crossed?

turns.agent_response:
Yes ‚Äî the simplest way is to give each thing a **different role, a different name, and a different place**.

A clean setup could be:

### 1) Main set
- Keep the active/official labels in one clearly named folder or box:
  - `MAIN`
  - `ACTIVE`
  - `CURRENT`
- Only this set is used for day-to-day work.

### 2) Backups
- Store backups separately and make them obviously not for daily use:
  - `BACKUP`
  - `ARCHIVE`
  - `OLD_VERSIONS`
- If possible, add dates:
  - `BACKUP_2026-04-29`

### 3) Notes
- Keep notes in a separate document or notebook:
  - `NOTES`
  - `LABEL_GUIDE`
  - `CHANGE_LOG`
- Don‚Äôt mix notes into the label set itself if you can avoid it.

### 4) Use a simple naming rule
For example:
- `project_main`
- `project_backup_01`
- `project_notes`

Or if you want something even harder to confuse:
- `A_MAIN`
- `B_BACKUP`
- `C_NOTES`

### 5) Add one visible marker
Use a color or tag system:
- Main = green
- Backup = gray
- Notes = blue

### 6) Keep a tiny log
A short change log helps prevent accidental swaps:
- date
- what changed
- where the latest version is

Example:
- `2026-04-29: Updated main labels. Backup saved as project_backup_2026-04-29.`

If you want, I can also help you turn this into a **very simple folder structure** or a **one-line naming convention** you can reuse every time.

user_simulator.text:
Mostly fine, but it‚Äôs still a little generic. I want something I can actually use without second-guessing myself later.

Give me a tighter scheme with:
- one naming pattern for main / backup / notes
- where each one physically lives
- what gets updated first, and what never gets overwritten
- a rule for duplicate labels so they don‚Äôt end up in the same folder by accident

Keep it simple. I don‚Äôt need a lecture. Just a clean layout I can follow every time.

turns.agent_response:
Use this every time:

## Naming pattern
`[PROJECT]_[ROLE]_[YYYY-MM-DD]`

Examples:
- `LabelSet_MAIN_2026-04-29`
- `LabelSet_BACKUP_2026-04-29`
- `LabelSet_NOTES_2026-04-29`

If you want it even harder to mix up, keep the role in all caps and never vary it.

## Physical layout
- **Main** ‚Üí one working folder only: `01_MAIN`
- **Backup** ‚Üí separate folder: `02_BACKUP`
- **Notes** ‚Üí separate folder or file: `03_NOTES`

Do not store notes inside `01_MAIN`.

## Update order
1. Update **MAIN**
2. Save a copy to **BACKUP**
3. Update **NOTES** last

## Never overwrite
- **BACKUP**: never edit in place
- **NOTES**: append only, don‚Äôt replace old entries
- **MAIN**: only this one gets active edits

## Duplicate label rule
If a label already exists, don‚Äôt put the new one in the same folder.

Use this rule:
- **Same label, different version** ‚Üí move to `02_BACKUP`
- **Same label, same purpose** ‚Üí keep only the newest in `01_MAIN`
- **Anything uncertain** ‚Üí goes to `02_BACKUP`, not `01_MAIN`

## Simple daily flow
- Put the current working set in `01_MAIN`
- Save a dated copy in `02_BACKUP`
- Keep changes and decisions in `03_NOTES`

If you want, I can turn this into a 3-line checklist you can paste into your workflow notes.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but I want it a little stricter. The part I still don‚Äôt like is the duplicate-label rule ‚Äî ‚Äúanything uncertain goes to backup‚Äù is fine, but I want a hard cutoff for when a label is considered a duplicate versus a new item. Also, notes should be append-only, yes, but I‚Äôd rather have a rule that says where the notes file lives so it doesn‚Äôt drift into the working folder.

Give me the same scheme, just tighter:
- one exact naming pattern
- one exact folder layout
- one exact trigger for backup
- one exact trigger for duplicate vs new

No extra explanation. Just the finished version I can use.

turns.agent_response:
Use this exact scheme:

## Naming pattern
`[PROJECT]_[ROLE]_[YYYY-MM-DD]`

Examples:
- `LabelSet_MAIN_2026-04-29`
- `LabelSet_BACKUP_2026-04-29`
- `LabelSet_NOTES_2026-04-29`

## Folder layout
- `01_MAIN/` ‚Üí active working set only
- `02_BACKUP/` ‚Üí all dated copies and duplicates
- `03_NOTES/` ‚Üí notes file only

## Backup trigger
Create a backup **after every change to MAIN**.

## Duplicate vs new trigger
- **Duplicate** = same label text **and** same purpose as an existing label
- **New item** = different label text **or** different purpose

## Rules
- If it is a **duplicate**, put it in `02_BACKUP/`
- If it is a **new item**, put it in `01_MAIN/`
- `03_NOTES/LabelSet_NOTES_YYYY-MM-DD` stays only in `03_NOTES/`
- Never store notes in `01_MAIN/`
- Never overwrite a backup

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That works.

- Naming: `[PROJECT]_[ROLE]_[YYYY-MM-DD]`
- Layout: `01_MAIN/`, `02_BACKUP/`, `03_NOTES/`
- Backup trigger: after every change to `MAIN`
- Duplicate trigger: same label text + same purpose = duplicate; anything else = new

Clean enough.
